reasoning: FSIS food recalls remain 'Active' until all effectiveness checks are completed and verified by the agency, which confirms that the recalling firm has notified its customers and removed the product from circulation. This administrative process usually takes several months. The timeline from April 24 to July 1 is roughly 67 days. Government regulatory records typically take longer than two months to officially close out moderately large (65,000 lbs) food recalls, especially given the bureaucratic nature of finalizing and publishing closed statuses.
